task_name: Uterine Vessel Coagulation (VirtaMed EDU - Surgical Simulation)
note: This video is a surgical simulation tutorial on uterine vessel coagulation, not an FLS (Fundamentals of Laparoscopic Surgery) task. FLS tasks are limited to: (1) Peg Transfer, (2) Pattern Cut, (3) Endoloop, (4) Extracorporeal Suture, and (5) Intracorporeal Suture. This content demonstrates advanced gynecological surgical technique using the VirtaMed simulator platform with instructional overlays, step-by-step guidance, and equipment selection menus visible throughout.
why_not_scored: VirtaMed surgical simulations are not part of the official FLS curriculum. This demonstrates a different surgical skill (hemostasis and vessel management in gynecology) using a different simulation platform and assessment framework. No FLS scoring rubric applies.

demonstrated_techniques: Exposure and mobilization of uterine vessels
Blunt and sharp dissection for vessel identification
Safe vessel sealing close to uterus to minimize bleeding
Tool selection (bipolar grasper, unipolar grasper, scissors, suction/irrigation)
verbal_cues: Make sure to expose and mobilize the uterine artery and vein at the ascending portion using blunt and sharp dissection until it can be safely identified.
Sufficiently coagulate and cut the left uterine vessels close to the uterus (about 1cm). Make sure not to cause any bleeding.
common_errors_addressed: Inadequate exposure of vessels before coagulation
Excessive force during dissection risking tissue injury
Coagulation too far from uterus increasing bleeding risk
Tool selection errors for specific dissection vs. coagulation phases

confidence_rationale: Content is clearly identified as VirtaMed EDU simulator with on-screen tutorial overlays, step progression indicators, and equipment menus. This is unambiguously not an FLS task.
